channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Wisconsin man charged with arson of congressman’s office over TikTok ban
item.description: Prosecutors charged a Wisconsin man who allegedly told police he tried to burn down a congressman’s office because he was upset with the federal TikTok ban with multiple counts on Wednesday, including arson.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Musk clashes with OpenAI CEO Sam Altman over Trump-supported Stargate AI data center project
item.description: Elon Musk is clashing with OpenAI CEO Sam Altman over the Stargate artificial intelligence infrastructure project touted by President Donald Trump, the latest in a feud between the two tech billionaires that started on OpenAI's board and is now testing Musk's influence with the new president.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: ByteDance releases new AI reasoning model in a bid to challenge OpenAI
item.description: TikTok owner ByteDance on Wednesday released an update to its flagship AI model aimed at challenging Microsoft-backed OpenAI’s latest reasoning model products, as a global race intensified to create AI models capable of tackling complex problems.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: Cisco CEO Robbins on AI: Pressure to deploy is real
item.description: Interest in AI has exploded over the last two or three years, but enterprises are only just beginning to think about how they’re going to take advantage of it, Cisco CEO Chuck Robbins told the audience at the company’s recent AI Summit.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Apple gender-discrimination lawsuit that allegedly started with man’s tax form left on printer gets boost from judge
item.description: Apple -- worth more than $3 trillion in the stock market and led by CEO Tim Cook, who received $74.6 million in pay last year -- sought to kill the lawsuit.
item.dc:creator: Ethan Baron, The Mercury News

item.title: Amazon to close 7 warehouses in the Canadian province of Quebec and eliminate 1,700 jobs
item.description: Online retailer Amazon said Wednesday that it's closing all seven of its warehouses in the Canadian province of Quebec in the next two months. The e-commerce giant said the move would provide “even more savings to our customers over the long run.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What Intel needs to do to get its mojo back
item.description: While 2024 was a banner year for some chip companies – notably Nvidia and Broadcom – Intel had a year it would rather forget. Another CEO was forced out, it lost market share, and its stock dropped 60% during a year that was strong for semiconductors.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Musk clashes with OpenAI CEO Sam Altman over Trump-supported Stargate AI data center project
item.description: It's the latest clash in a feud between the two tech billionaires that started on OpenAI’s board and is now testing Musk’s influence with the new president.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: We celebrate the Samsung Galaxy S25 launch event with a special episode of our podcast
item.description: Let's talk the new Samsung Galaxy S25, and everything else that was announced at Samsung's Unpacked 2025 event.
item.dc:creator: hamish.hector@futurenet.com (Hamish Hector), TechRadar

item.title: MrBeast not quite in the bidding race for TikTok just yet, rep says
item.description: Jimmy Donaldson — better known online as MrBeast — isn’t in the TikTok bidding race just yet, according to a representative for the YouTube star.
item.dc:creator: James Pollard, Haleluya Hadero And Wyatte Grantham-Philips, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: John Malkovich and The Bear’s Ayo Edebiri get swept up in a pop music cult in first trailer for A24’s Opus – and it's gone straight to the top of my watch list
item.description: A24 has dropped a thrilling first trailer for music horror Opus, and it's gone straight to the top of my 2025 watchlist. Watch the first full trailer here.
item.dc:creator: rowan.davies@futurenet.com (Rowan Davies), TechRadar

item.title: 5 practical tips for emerging IT leaders
item.description: When they announced my name on stage at CIO Summit and Awards 2024 as the winner for Emerging ICT Leader of the Year award, my profound disbelief and shock flared up my impostor syndrome one more time as I walked up the stage.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Perplexity Launches Sonar API: A New Player in Generative AI Search Technology
item.description: Perplexity has introduced an API service named Sonar that allows developers and enterprises to embed the company's generative AI search technology into their applications.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: AI experiment in halfpipe judging at X Games will give snowboarders a glimpse into the future
item.description: The X Games will experiment judging halfpipe runs this week in Aspen using artificial intelligence, the cutting-edge technology that could someday play a role in the way subjectively judged sports are scored.
item.dc:creator: EDDIE PELLS, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Sustainability, grid demands, AI workloads will challenge data center growth in 2025
item.description: Data centers this year will face several challenges as the demand for artificial intelligence introduces an evolution in AI hardware, on-premises and cloud-based strategies for training and inference, and innovations in power distributions—all while opposition to new data center developments continu
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: 10 tech upgrades to save your time, privacy and money this year
item.description: Today's tech can help make life so much easier — if you know how to use it. Here are a few tips on the quickest and best ways to make your phone, laptop and other devices work for you.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Auto-followed Trump on Facebook? It's probably because you previously liked the POTUS account
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Some Facebook and Instagram users were surprised to find themselves automatically following the accounts of President Donald Trump or Vice President JD Vance this week after the president was inaugurated — but that's likely because they had previously followed official U.S.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Does your Windows 11 PC keep restarting? Let's fix that frustrating problem
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson offers Windows 11 restart fixes: Update drivers, run system scans and check hardware.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Sony’s surprising entry into electric vehicle market
item.description: Sony and Honda's Afeela 1 EV launches with advanced tech and entertainment for $89,900. Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson gives his takeaways.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Fake job interview emails installing hidden cryptocurrency mining malware
item.description: Scammers have a new trick where they pretend to be job recruiters to plant malicious apps on your devices. It all starts with a deceptive email.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Who could buy TikTok? Only a few parties are serious about offering
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The supremely popular TikTok could be banned on Jan. 19 under a federal law that forces the video sharing platform to divest itself from its China-based parent company, ByteDance, or shut down its U.S. operations.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Bitcoin soars past $100,000 ahead of possible early action on crypto by Trump
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The price of bitcoin topped $100,000 again early Friday as a pumped up cryptocurrency industry expects early action by Donald Trump when he's sworn in as president next week.
item.dc:creator: ALAN SUDERMAN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Easterly hails CISA’s ‘world-class’ talent in final address
item.description: CISA grew rapidly during the Biden administration, recruiting technical experts to lead key initiatives like "secure by design" and cyberthreat hunting teams. The post Easterly hails CISA’s ‘world-class’ talent in final address first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: SASE 2025: Impact grows despite adoption hurdles
item.description: Secure access service edge technologies will garner a lot of attention in 2025, according to a recent survey, but despite the many benefits SASE promises, network and security leaders face an equal number of challenges in successfully implementing the technology.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Execs are prioritizing skills over degrees
item.description: When considering new hires, 80% of corporate executives will prioritize skills over degrees, with half planning to increase freelance hiring this year to fill in for a gap in AI and other skills, according to a new study from freelancing platform Upwork.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Snowflake open sources SwiftKV to reduce inference workload costs
item.description: Cloud-based data warehouse company Snowflake has open-sourced a new proprietary approach — SwiftKV — designed to reduce the cost of inference workloads for enterprises running generative AI-based applications. SwiftKV was launched in December.
item.dc:creator: Info World
